parsed_review.summary: CP00-692 read-only review: PASS. Descriptor-only RP22 External Integrations I P09 permission/audit binding tail + synthetic fixture foundation bridge (10 units, risk A) correctly claiming production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false, awaiting this hardened read-only review. All descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries verified across registry.js, service.js (freezeCp692Result attestation), validators.js, and model.test.js; both contracts and the validator script consistent at CP00-692; Claude bounded read-only/non-final; Hermes bounded to control-plane packet/receipt only. Review-hardening preconditions met (auth preflight passed, valid JSON, no forbidden tools, prompt within size). One non-blocking P3 (coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot fallback, same accepted topology as CP00-690/691). No P0/P1/P2. Provenance caveat: 22 validation commands recorded exit 0 and consistent with inspected source, but not independently re-executed under read-only constraints.

findings.title: Live pack coverage resolved via manifest plan_binding_snapshot rather than an independent plan-array entry
findings.details: The live docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json carries CP00-692 only in live_state (line 36); its packs[] array begins at CP00-693 (line 402). As a result both the contract-validation script (validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:373-375) and the model test (packages/integrations-core/test/model.test.js:362-364) resolve the CP00-692 plan pack through the `?? cp692Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot` fallback, so the live pack's coverage is checked against a snapshot embedded in the pack's own manifest rather than an independent plan-array entry. Risk is low: validateExternalIntegrationsICp692Coverage (validators.js:1079-1097) checks that snapshot against the frozen EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP692_REQUIREMENTS constants hard-coded in registry.js:1777-1830 (deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts, independently re-verified to sum to 10), which serve as the code-reviewed anchor, and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's source_ledger_sha (13c7ed65...) indicating common generator provenance. This is the same topology recorded as a non-blocking P3 in the CP00-690 and CP00-691 reviews.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Optionally, a future generator run could emit the current/live pack into plan.packs[] (or a dedicated closed-packs array) so the live pack's coverage is cross-validated against a plan-level entry independent of its own manifest.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as expected, low-risk design; non-blocking. The frozen REQUIREMENTS constants in registry.js are the independent coverage anchor and the manifest snapshot shares the live plan's ledger SHA, so the verification chain remains sound.
